title: check_network_guard aborts chat instead of disabling wallet
description: chat() calls check_network_guard(data_dir, network)? which propagates Err and kills the entire session on network mismatch. A user with a testnet wallet who runs 'nie --network mainnet chat' cannot join the room at all, even though chat has no wallet dependency. The guard should disable wallet features (return None from try_load_wallet_fvks) rather than abort the session. Fix: move the network check inside try_load_wallet_fvks — if meta.network \!= network, print a warning, return None (wallet disabled for session). Chat continues without payment features instead of refusing to start.
close_reason: Fixed: moved network check into try_load_wallet_fvks(expected_network). Network mismatch now prints a warning and returns None (wallet disabled for session) instead of aborting chat() entirely. Test updated to pass 'testnet' as expected_network.
